2603.17887.title: AI-Assisted Goal Setting Improves Goal Progress Through Social Accountability
2603.17887.paper_summary: Helping people identify and pursue personally meaningful career goals at scale remains a key challenge in applied psychology. Career coaching can improve goal quality and attainment, but its cost and limited availability restrict access. Large language model (LLM)-based chatbots offer a scalable alternative, yet the psychological mechanisms by which they might support goal pursuit remain untested. Here we report a preregistered three-arm randomised controlled trial (N = 517) comparing an AI career coach ("Leon," powered by Claude Sonnet), a matched structured written questionnaire covering closely matched reflective topics, and a no-support control on goal progress at a two-week follow-up. The AI chatbot produced significantly higher goal progress than the control (d = 0.33, p = .016). Compared with the written-reflection condition, the AI did not significantly improve overall goal progress, but it increased perceived social accountability. In the preregistered mediation model, perceived accountability mediated the AI-over-questionnaire effect on goal progress (indirect effect = 0.15, 95% CI [0.04, 0.31]), whereas self-concordance did not. These findings suggest that AI-assisted goal setting can improve short-term goal progress, and that its clearest added value over structured self-reflection lies in increasing felt accountability.
2603.17887.paper_summary_zh: 幫助人們在大規模上識別和追求個人意義的職業目標仍然是應用心理學中的一個主要挑戰。職業輔導可以改善目標質量和達成度，但其成本和有限的可用性限制了訪問。基於大型語言模型（LLM）的聊天機器人提供了一種可擴展的替代方案，但它們可能支持目標追求的心理機制仍未經過測試。在這裡，我們報告了一項預註冊的三臂隨機對照試驗（N = 517），比較了一個AI職業教練（“Leon”，由Claude Sonnet提供支持）、一個涵蓋密切相關反思主題的匹配結構化書面問卷，以及一個無支持的對照組在兩週後的目標進展。AI聊天機器人的目標進展顯著高於對照組（d = 0.33，p = .016）。與書面反思條件相比，AI並未顯著改善整體目標進展，但它增加了感知的社會問責感。在預註冊的中介模型中，感知的問責感在AI與問卷效果對目標進展的影響中起到了中介作用（間接效應 = 0.15，95% CI [0.04, 0.31]），而自我一致性則沒有。這些發現表明，AI輔助的目標設定可以改善短期目標進展，其對結構化自我反思的最明顯附加價值在於增加感受到的問責感。
2603.17887.author: Michel Schimpf et.al.
2603.17887.authors: Michel Schimpf, Julian Voigt, Thomas Bohné
